concerns.text: Phase 7 (Reviewer-Driven Enhancements) introduces tasks T036-T048 implementing new metrics (coherence_score, ground_truth_accuracy, memory_fidelity) and mechanisms (forgetting, noise, consolidation) that are NOT present in spec.md's Functional Requirements (FR-001 to FR-012). This constitutes unapproved scope creep and violates the Single Source of Truth principle (Constitution Principle IV) by generating data without corresponding spec requirements.

Task T020 describes implementing a 'single two-way independent-samples ANOVA' on a 'combined DataFrame'. While the task notes this resolves a plan contradiction, the phrasing risks conflating the design. FR-006 explicitly requires a 'two-way independent-samples ANOVA with factors Context and Metric' to test the interaction. The task description should explicitly mandate the two-way layout (Context × Metric) to ensure the interaction term is correctly computed as required by the spec, rather than implying a single aggregated test.

Task T022 instructs to 'sweep token thresholds across a range of values'. FR-008 explicitly mandates sweeping the specific set {128, 256, 512} tokens. The task description lacks this specificity, creating a risk that the implementer will choose an arbitrary range rather than the required discrete set, violating the acceptance criteria.

Tasks T046 and T047 in Phase 7 pivot the scaling analysis to focus on 'memory_fidelity' and 'communication_overhead'. SC-005 and US-3 explicitly require the scaling plot to show trends for 'specialization index and retrieval efficiency'. Replacing these required metrics with new, unapproved ones in the final deliverable violates the success criteria.

Task T015 specifies outputting results for a 'substantial set of games'. User Story 1 and FR-009 explicitly define the scale as '1,000 games' for statistical power analysis. The task description should be precise (1,000 games) to avoid ambiguity in the deliverable's statistical validity and ensure compliance with the power analysis requirements.
